title: United Nations University (UNU) Careers 2026: Apply for Three Exciting Global Opportunities in Research, Artificial Intelligence, Education, and Sustainable Development
summary: The United Nations University (UNU) Macau has announced applications for three outstanding international opportunities for researchers, educators, and professionals passionate about leveraging digital technologies to advance sustainable development. The available opportunities include the Visiting Research Fellowship (2026) , Training Educational Analyst , and Senior Researcher – Team Lead . These positions provide talented professionals and researchers with the opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge work in artificial intelligence, digital innovation, education, and sustainable development within the United Nations system. Whether you are an emerging researcher looking to collaborate on impactful projects, an education professional interested in designing innovative learning programmes, or an experienced research leader ready to shape global AI and digital policy, UNU offers a unique platform to make a global impact. About the United Nations University (UNU) The United Nations University is the academic and research arm of the United Nations. For more than five decades, UNU has generated policy-relevant research and educational initiatives addressing global challenges related to sustainable development, technology, governance, climate change, and human welfare. UNU Macau focuses on Digital Emerging Technologies for Sustainable Development (DET4SD) , exploring how artificial intelligence, digital innovation, and emerging technologies can support evidence-based policymaking and accelerate progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). 1. Visiting Research Fellowship (2026) The Visiting Research Fellowship provides researchers with the opportunity to work directly alongside UNU Macau experts on innovative projects exploring the intersection of digital technologies and sustainable development. The fellowship welcomes both early-career researchers and senior experts who can contribute innovative ideas and practical solutions that support governments and United Nations agencies. Fellowship Areas Research proposals may include topics such as: Artificial Intelligence Digital Governance Emerging Technologies Sustainable Development Public Policy Responsible AI Data-driven decision making Digital inclusion Expected Outputs Selected fellows will contribute through: Collaborative research Development of measurable applications Active engagement with UNU research teams Knowledge sharing Policy-oriented research outputs The fellowship typically lasts one to three months between September and December 2026 , with financial support provided to help offset travel and living costs in Macau. 2. Training Educational Analyst UNU Macau is also recruiting a Training Educational Analyst to strengthen its educational programmes and capacity-building initiatives. The successful candidate will support the design, delivery, and evaluation of innovative learning experiences while contributing to research and organizational development. Key Responsibilities Responsibilities include: Designing training programmes Facilitating learning activities Coordinating with stakeholders Evaluating learning effectiveness Preparing educational materials Supporting organizational development Conducting research aligned with institutional priorities Performing additional duties assigned by management This position is ideal for professionals passionate about education, instructional design, digital learning, and international capacity development. 3. Senior Researcher – Team Lead The Senior Researcher – Team Lead position is designed for experienced researchers capable of leading multidisciplinary research teams and delivering internationally recognized research. The successful candidate will lead research activities while building partnerships, supervising projects, mentoring researchers, and representing UNU in international academic and policy forums. Responsibilities Key responsibilities include: Leading high-quality research programmes Managing multidisciplinary research teams Building international partnerships Mobilizing research resources Publishing impactful research Presenting at international conferences Leading educational and training activities Supporting institutional strategy and innovation This role is well suited for accomplished academics and professionals with expertise in AI, emerging technologies, digital governance, and sustainable development. Why Apply? These UNU opportunities provide exceptional professional and academic benefits, including: Working within the United Nations system Contributing to global sustainable development initiatives Collaborating with leading international researchers Building expertise in artificial intelligence and digital innovation Expanding international professional networks Contributing to policy-relevant research Supporting governments and UN agencies with evidence-based solutions Application Deadlines Applicants should take note of the following deadlines: Visiting Research Fellowship: 24 July 2026 Training Educational Analyst: 2 August 2026 Senior Researcher – Team Lead: 9 August 2026 How to Apply Interested applicants should review the detailed requirements for their preferred opportunity and submit their applications through the official United Nations University Careers portal. Application links: Visiting Research Fellowship: Apply Here Training Educational Analyst: Apply Here Senior Researcher – Team Lead: Apply Here Conclusion The United Nations University Macau s latest recruitment presents an exciting opportunity for researchers, educators, and professionals eager to contribute to the future of artificial intelligence, digital transformation, and sustainable development. Whether you are seeking a research fellowship or a professional career within the United Nations system, these opportunities provide an excellent platform to work on globally significant projects while collaborating with world-class experts. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply before the respective deadlines. For more information on these opportunities, visit here. Discover more global opportunities on OFY, visit here. The post United Nations University (UNU) Careers 2026: Apply for Three Exciting Global Opportunities in Research, Artificial Intelligence, Education, and Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Gulbenkian Institute Scholarships for Advanced Study 2026–2027 in Portugal | Fully Funded Research Residency for Internationally Renowned Academics, Scholars, and Artists
summary: Researchers, distinguished academics, and accomplished scholars seeking uninterrupted time for high-quality independent research now have an exceptional opportunity to apply for the Gulbenkian Institute Scholarships for Advanced Study 2026–2027 . Offered by the Calouste Gulbenkian Foundation through the Gulbenkian Institute for Advanced Study (IGEA) , this prestigious international scholarship programme provides selected fellows with the opportunity to undertake independent research in Lisbon, Portugal, for a period of three to eight months . Designed to foster intellectual excellence, interdisciplinary dialogue, and original scholarship, the programme brings together internationally recognized academics, independent researchers, emeritus professors, and artists from around the world. Successful applicants will join a vibrant scholarly community where they can pursue innovative research projects in a stimulating environment with minimal administrative or teaching obligations. Applications for the 2026–2027 intake are open until 15 October 2026 (3:00 PM Lisbon time) . About the Gulbenkian Institute for Advanced Study (IGEA) The Gulbenkian Institute for Advanced Study (IGEA) is an initiative of the Calouste Gulbenkian Foundation , one of Europe s leading philanthropic organizations dedicated to advancing knowledge, culture, education, and scientific inquiry. The Institute provides scholars with an environment specifically designed to support: Independent thinking Deep intellectual reflection Interdisciplinary collaboration Original academic research High-quality scholarly output Rather than functioning as a conventional university department, the Institute creates an academic community where fellows from different disciplines, countries, and intellectual traditions interact, exchange ideas, and contribute to a rich culture of research and innovation. The programme emphasizes intellectual freedom, allowing fellows to focus almost exclusively on their proposed research while engaging with a diverse network of internationally respected scholars. About the Scholarships The Gulbenkian Institute Scholarships for Advanced Study are intended to support internationally recognized academics and researchers undertaking original, independent research projects. Each fellowship provides scholars with a temporary academic residency at the Institute, enabling them to advance significant research projects in an intellectually stimulating environment with very few routine responsibilities. The scholarship is particularly suited to scholars who require dedicated time for: Writing books or monographs Completing major research projects Developing theoretical frameworks Advancing interdisciplinary studies Producing high-impact scholarly publications Conducting archival or library-based research Engaging in intellectual collaboration Residencies typically last between three and eight months , depending on the proposed project. Purpose of the Programme The Institute aims to create an international community of exceptional scholars by providing an environment where participants can: Conduct independent research. Develop innovative ideas. Engage in interdisciplinary dialogue. Exchange knowledge with leading academics. Produce original scholarly work. Advance international research collaboration. Strengthen global academic networks. Contribute to intellectual debates across disciplines. Rather than emphasizing practical or applied projects, the programme prioritizes reflective, theoretical, and intellectually ambitious research. Scholarship Duration Successful applicants may receive support for a residency lasting: Three months Four months Five months Six months Seven months Eight months The exact duration depends on the approved research proposal and residency plan. Research Environment One of the defining features of the programme is the exceptional academic environment offered by the Institute. During their residency, fellows benefit from: Quiet research spaces. Dedicated time for independent scholarship. Access to an international intellectual community. Opportunities for interdisciplinary discussions. Academic exchange with researchers from diverse fields. Freedom from regular teaching obligations. A supportive atmosphere for reflection and creative thinking. The Institute intentionally limits routine responsibilities so fellows can devote their attention to advancing their research. Fields of Research Applications are welcomed from most academic disciplines , provided the proposed research: Does not require laboratory facilities. Does not depend on experimental scientific infrastructure. Is not predominantly practical or applied in nature. Examples of suitable disciplines include: Humanities History Literature Philosophy Classics Languages Religious Studies Cultural Studies Social Sciences Political Science Sociology Anthropology Economics International Relations Human Geography Arts Art History Musicology Visual Arts Cultural Heritage Creative Research Cognitive and Language Sciences Linguistics Cognitive Linguistics Communication Studies Semiotics Archaeology Archaeological Research Heritage Studies Ancient Civilizations Other Eligible Disciplines The programme also welcomes researchers working across interdisciplinary fields where independent scholarly research is the central focus. Intellectual Community A major strength of the Institute is its diverse scholarly community. Fellows come from: Different countries Different academic traditions Multiple disciplines Universities worldwide Independent research institutions This diversity creates opportunities for: Cross-disciplinary collaboration. Informal academic dialogue. Intellectual exchange. New research partnerships. Broader scholarly perspectives. Selection Process Applications undergo a highly competitive international review process. Evaluation is conducted by the IGEA Advisory Board . Applications are assessed based on: Academic excellence. Originality of the proposed project. Research quality. Intellectual significance. Suitability for the Institute s research environment. Contribution to the interdisciplinary scholarly community. Only proposals demonstrating outstanding scholarly merit will be selected. Eligibility Requirements The Institute seeks applications from internationally accomplished scholars. Eligible applicants include: Internationally renowned academics. Active university faculty members. Emeritus professors. Independent scholars. Artists engaged in scholarly research. Applicants should have established records of academic achievement and demonstrate the ability to undertake independent, high-quality research. Who Should Apply? The scholarship is particularly suitable for: Professors. Associate Professors. Assistant Professors. Senior Researchers. Independent Researchers. Academic Authors. Historians. Philosophers. Political Scientists. Linguists. Archaeologists. Social Scientists. Arts Researchers. Distinguished Scholars. Application Requirements Applicants must submit an individual application. The application should include: 1. Curriculum Vitae (CV) Applicants should provide an updated academic CV highlighting: Academic qualifications Employment history Publications Awards Research achievements Professional experience 2. Research Proposal Applicants must provide a brief description of: Proposed research activities. Objectives. Planned work during the residency. Expected scholarly outcomes. 3. Referees Applicants should provide: Names of two academic referees. Letters of recommendation may subsequently be requested from these referees. Application Process Applications are submitted individually through the Gulbenkian Online Application System . Applicants must first create a MyGulbenkian account before completing the application. Candidates should ensure all required documentation is uploaded before submission. Evaluation Criteria Applications are judged according to: Academic excellence. Originality. Innovation. Research quality. Intellectual contribution. Suitability for the Institute. Potential contribution to interdisciplinary scholarship. Scholarship Results Scholarship offers will begin to be issued: From the end of January of the following year. Successful candidates will receive formal notification after completion of the international evaluation process. Previous Fellows The Institute regularly hosts distinguished scholars from leading universities worldwide. Examples of recent fellows include academics affiliated with: Yale University Columbia University University of Michigan Texas Tech University University of Bristol Leiden University Koç University Bard College University of Missouri Europeana Foundation American University in Cairo Central European University Vilnius University These scholars represented disciplines such as: History Philosophy Political Science Musicology Literature Psychiatry Classical Studies Archaeology Arts Cognitive Linguistics The diversity of previous fellows demonstrates the Institute s commitment to interdisciplinary excellence. Benefits of the Scholarship Successful fellows will enjoy numerous professional and academic advantages, including: Residency at the prestigious Gulbenkian Institute. Dedicated time for research. Access to an international scholarly community. Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration. Intellectual exchange with leading academics. Supportive research environment. Minimal routine obligations. Enhanced international visibility. Strengthened academic networks. Opportunity to produce high-quality scholarly publications. Important Dates Activity Date Applications Open 1 July 2026 Application Deadline 15 October 2026 (3:00 PM Lisbon Time) Scholarship Offers Begin End of January (following year) Why You Should Apply This programme is one of Europe s most prestigious advanced study residencies for established scholars. Applicants should consider applying because it offers: International prestige. Dedicated research time. Freedom to pursue ambitious scholarship. Exceptional interdisciplinary dialogue. Long-lasting academic collaborations. Access to one of Europe s leading intellectual communities. Whether completing a major publication, developing new theoretical frameworks, or advancing interdisciplinary research, fellows gain the time and environment necessary to produce work of lasting scholarly significance. Tips for Preparing a Strong Application Applicants can improve their competitiveness by: Clearly articulating the originality of their research. Demonstrating international scholarly impact. Explaining why the residency environment is essential for the project. Presenting a realistic work plan. Highlighting previous publications and research achievements. Selecting referees familiar with their academic contributions. Visit HERE for more information about this Scholarship. Conclusion The Gulbenkian Institute Scholarships for Advanced Study 2026–2027 represent a remarkable opportunity for internationally recognized academics, researchers, and artists to undertake independent scholarship in one of Europe s leading intellectual environments. By offering residencies of three to eight months, the programme enables scholars to focus exclusively on producing innovative, high-quality research while engaging with an international community of distinguished fellows. Hosted by the Calouste Gulbenkian Foundation in Lisbon, the programme supports interdisciplinary dialogue, intellectual freedom, and academic excellence across a broad range of disciplines. Researchers seeking uninterrupted time to advance significant scholarly work are encouraged to submit a strong application before the 15 October 2026 deadline. For more global Scholarship opportunities, visit HERE . The post Gulbenkian Institute Scholarships for Advanced Study 2026–2027 in Portugal | Fully Funded Research Residency for Internationally Renowned Academics, Scholars, and Artists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Bloomsbury Academic Writing Fellowship 2026: £1,000 Funding, One-Year Mentorship, Editorial Support and Publishing Opportunities for Early-Career Academics in the UK
summary: The Bloomsbury Academic Writing Fellowship 2026 is now accepting applications from early-career academics and researchers based in the United Kingdom who identify as Black, Asian or Ethnically Diverse (BAED) . Now in its fourth consecutive year, this prestigious fellowship is jointly delivered by Bloomsbury Academic and Writers Artists to support emerging scholars in transforming innovative academic research into publishable books. Designed to address barriers within academic publishing and promote greater diversity in scholarly voices, the fellowship provides a comprehensive package of editorial mentorship, financial support, networking opportunities, professional development, and publishing resources throughout 2027 . If you have an original book idea in the Humanities or Social Sciences and aspire to become a published academic author, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to develop your manuscript under the guidance of experienced publishing professionals and leading academics. About the Bloomsbury Academic Writing Fellowship The Bloomsbury Academic Writing Fellowship was established to help talented early-career scholars refine their academic book proposals and manuscripts until they are ready for submission to prospective publishers. Rather than simply providing financial assistance, the fellowship delivers an industry-leading programme of mentorship, editorial guidance, publishing insight, networking opportunities, and academic development designed to prepare scholars for long-term success in academic publishing. The fellowship particularly aims to amplify voices from historically underrepresented communities within academia and publishing. Fellowship Benefits Successful applicants will receive an extensive package of support throughout 2027 , including: Financial Support £1,000 financial award paid at the beginning of the fellowship year. Editorial Mentorship Recipients will benefit from: A bespoke one-year mentoring programme. Editorial guidance from experienced publishing professionals. Four milestone mentoring sessions throughout the fellowship. Individual feedback on manuscript development. Editorial reports after each mentoring meeting. At least one in-person mentoring meeting at Bloomsbury s London offices, where possible. Professional Development Successful fellows will receive: Registration to a UK-based academic conference attended by Bloomsbury. Complimentary enrolment in the Writing Non-Fiction five-week online course from Writers Artists. Free access to selected Writers Artists events during 2027. Invitations to Bloomsbury Academic author events. Opportunities to participate in Bloomsbury fellowship panels and recordings. A 30-minute one-to-one consultation with a literary agent, including proposal feedback and publishing advice. Publishing Resources The fellowship also includes: One-year access to the Writers Artists online industry listings database. £250 worth of Bloomsbury Academic books. Benefits for Runners-Up Shortlisted runners-up will receive: £250 worth of Bloomsbury Academic books. Access to one Writers Artists Evening Masterclass. Subscription access to the Writers Artists Listings database. Eligible Academic Subjects Applicants should propose manuscripts addressing important themes within the Humanities and Social Sciences. Eligible subject areas include: Gender and Sexuality Economics and International Development Social Welfare, Society and Communities Politics, Government and Current Affairs Equality and Diversity Climate and Environment History and Cultural Heritage Popular Culture and Media Literary Studies The fellowship encourages projects that are timely, original, socially relevant, and capable of educating and inspiring wider audiences. Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following: 1. Book Synopsis A 200-word synopsis explaining: The proposed book. Its main objectives. How it educates and inspires readers. Which eligible theme it addresses. 2. Provisional Table of Contents Include: Proposed chapter outline. Estimated total manuscript word count. 3. Sample Writing Submit: A manuscript extract between 1,000 and 1,500 words . Only the first 1,500 words will be reviewed by judges. 4. Writers Artists Account Applicants must create a free account on the Writers Artists website before submitting their application. 5. Online Application Form Applications must be submitted through the official online competition portal. Application Timeline Applications open: 1 July 2026 (9:00 a.m. UK Time) Application deadline: 1 August 2026 (11:59 p.m. UK Time) Successful applicants will be announced in: December 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare all supporting documents before the application window opens. Draft applications can be saved and completed later through the Writers Artists dashboard. Judging Panel Applications will be assessed by a distinguished panel comprising leading academics, researchers, publishing professionals, and literary experts, including: Claire Alexander Emily Drewe Elvis Imafidon Eli Keren Richard Kerridge Ulrika Maude Lisa Pine Kadian Pow Together, the panel brings expertise across sociology, philosophy, literature, history, publishing, environmental humanities, Black studies, and literary representation. Why Apply? The Bloomsbury Academic Writing Fellowship offers much more than funding. It provides emerging academics with the tools, mentorship, publishing knowledge, and professional networks needed to transform original research into a publishable academic book. Key advantages include: £1,000 financial support. One year of personalized editorial mentoring. Publishing guidance from Bloomsbury Academic. Networking with publishing professionals. Attendance at academic conferences. Professional writing courses. Literary agent consultation. Access to valuable publishing resources. Increased opportunity to secure a future publishing contract. Application Deadline Deadline: 1 August 2026 (11:59 p.m. UK Time) Late applications will not be considered. How to Apply Interested applicants should: Create a free Writers Artists account. Prepare the required synopsis, table of contents, and writing sample. Complete the online application form. Submit all required documents before 1 August 2026 . Apply Here Conclusion The Bloomsbury Academic Writing Fellowship 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for Black, Asian, and Ethnically Diverse early-career academics in the UK to receive world-class editorial mentorship, financial support, and publishing guidance. By nurturing diverse scholarly voices and helping researchers develop publishable manuscripts, the fellowship continues to play an important role in creating a more inclusive academic publishing landscape. If you have an original book idea that can contribute to conversations across the Humanities and Social Sciences, this fellowship could be the ideal next step in your academic publishing journey. For more information on this fellowship opportunity, visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY, here The post Bloomsbury Academic Writing Fellowship 2026: £1,000 Funding, One-Year Mentorship, Editorial Support and Publishing Opportunities for Early-Career Academics in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 2027 Purpose Earth Grant Program: Receive Up to $10,000 in Funding and Mentorship for Environmental, Community, and Cultural Projects
summary: The Purpose Earth Grant Program has officially opened applications for its 2027 funding cycle, offering purpose-driven leaders and organizations an opportunity to receive grant funding of up to USD $10,000 , together with professional mentorship and global recognition. The initiative is designed to support innovative projects that create meaningful and lasting change within communities by addressing environmental challenges, strengthening community development, and promoting cultural collaboration. Applications are accepted on a first-come, first-served basis, with only the first 200 fully completed applications being considered. If the application cap is not reached earlier, the submission window will close on 10 August 2026 . About the Purpose Earth Grant Program Purpose Earth is an international grant and mentorship initiative dedicated to empowering individuals and organizations that are developing practical solutions to some of today s most pressing global challenges. Rather than simply providing financial assistance, the program seeks to partner with visionary leaders by combining funding, mentorship, and promotional opportunities to help projects grow sustainably and maximize their long-term impact. Each year, the organization invites applications from individuals and organizations whose work positively transforms local and global communities. Areas of Focus Projects submitted must align with at least one of the following priority areas. 1. Environmental Protection Projects should focus on protecting or restoring the environment through activities such as: Climate change solutions Conservation initiatives Environmental restoration Sustainable resource management Biodiversity protection Reducing environmental degradation 2. Community Activation Purpose Earth supports initiatives that strengthen communities by: Building resilient community networks Encouraging civic participation Promoting social innovation Improving quality of life Creating long-term positive social change 3. Cultural Collaboration Applicants may also submit projects that: Encourage creative expression Preserve cultural heritage Build connections across communities Foster compassion, inclusion, and collaboration Promote cross-cultural understanding What Successful Grant Recipients Will Receive Recipients selected for the 2027 Purpose Earth Grant Program will benefit from several forms of support beyond financial assistance. Grant Funding Successful applicants may receive funding ranging from USD $2,000 to USD $10,000 , depending on the needs and scope of their project. Professional Mentorship Awardees will receive personalized mentorship from experienced professionals who volunteer their expertise to help projects succeed. Mentorship may include guidance on: Project implementation Leadership Strategic planning Community engagement Organizational growth Long-term sustainability Global Visibility Grant recipients will also receive promotional support through Purpose Earth s communication channels, including: Featured project pages on the Purpose Earth website Digital media promotion Increased international exposure Participation in community celebrations and recognition events Who Can Apply? Purpose Earth welcomes applications from: Individual changemakers Community leaders Non-profit organizations Social enterprises Mission-driven organizations Environmental organizations Community development initiatives Cultural organizations Applicants must demonstrate that their project has a clear plan for implementation and will generate measurable community impact. APPLY HERE Funding Requirements Applicants should note the following funding conditions: Maximum funding available is USD $10,000 . Applications requesting more than USD $10,000 will not be considered. A detailed budget is mandatory. Applicants must clearly explain how every dollar of requested funding will be used. Projects That Will Not Receive Funding Purpose Earth does not provide funding for: Salaries or wages Consultant fees Contractor payments Advisor compensation Operational support Travel expenses Capital campaigns Sponsorships Conferences Prize programs Projects whose total cost greatly exceeds the maximum grant amount where Purpose Earth s contribution would only represent a small fraction of the overall budget. Eligibility Requirements Before applying, applicants should ensure they meet all eligibility requirements. Projects must: Still be active or in development. Not already be completed. Align with Purpose Earth s mission. Demonstrate community impact. Include a realistic implementation timeline. Include a detailed budget. Be submitted entirely in English. Supporting documents, including professional reference letters, must also be written in English. Videos should preferably be presented in English or include English subtitles. Professional Reference Letter Requirements Each application must include at least one professional reference letter. The reference letter must: Be dated within the last year. Come from someone who has worked professionally with the applicant. Clearly explain the professional relationship. Mention the project or similar work completed together. Support the applicant s qualifications. Include the referee s website or professional social media profile, such as LinkedIn. Personal references without professional relevance will not be accepted. Video Submission Requirements A one-minute video is a mandatory component of the application. The video should: Introduce the applicant. Explain the project s vision. Describe the expected impact. Show how the Purpose Earth grant would help achieve project goals. Professional production quality is not required. Applicants may record their video using a smartphone. The video link must remain accessible through one of the following methods: YouTube (Public or Unlisted) Google Drive Vimeo Dropbox WeTransfer (while ensuring the link remains active) Videos marked as private or requiring viewing permission may result in an incomplete application. Application Review Process Purpose Earth conducts a comprehensive review process to ensure every selected project meets its standards. Applications undergo careful evaluation and verification, including compliance with applicable financial regulations and sanctions screening. The organization also verifies that grant funding will be used appropriately and that applicants represent legitimate organizations or initiatives. For more information, visit 2027 Purpose Earth Grant Program HERE Grant Timeline Applicants should take note of the following schedule: Applications Open: 8 July 2026 Application Deadline: 10 August 2026 , or once 200 fully completed applications have been received. August 2026: Applications close. September to December 2026: Grant Selection Council reviews and vets applications. January to February 2027: Successful applicants receive award notifications. February 2027: First grant installment is paid. April to May 2027: Public announcement of recipients and online celebration. May 2027: Second grant installment is paid. Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on several important factors, including: Alignment with Purpose Earth s mission. Sustainability of the project. Potential for lasting community impact. Innovation and creativity. Quality of project planning. Strength of implementation strategy. Applicant experience and leadership. Realistic project timeline. Contribution to environmental protection, community activation, or cultural collaboration. Important Notes for Applicants Applicants should carefully review every application requirement before submitting. Keep the following in mind: Only fully completed applications will be reviewed. AI-generated applications are not accepted. Only one application may be submitted per project. Supporting documents must be submitted in English. Ensure every required attachment is included before submission. Applications cannot be edited after submission. Because only the first 200 complete applications will be accepted, interested applicants are encouraged to prepare all required documents—including their budget, reference letter, and one-minute video—before beginning the application process. For more Global opportunities, visit OFY HERE Deadline Applications close on 10 August 2026 , or earlier if the maximum limit of 200 fully completed applications is rueached. Conclusion The 2027 Purpose Earth Grant Program represents an outstanding opportunity for individuals and organizations working to create meaningful environmental, community, and cultural change. Beyond financial support of up to USD $10,000, recipients gain access to experienced mentors, international visibility, and a global network committed to creating lasting impact. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review every eligibility requirement, prepare all supporting documentation in advance, and submit a complete application as early as possible due to the limited number of applications that will be accepted. Projects with a clear vision, measurable community impact, sustainable implementation strategy, and strong alignment with Purpose Earth s mission will be well positioned during the competitive selection process. The post 2027 Purpose Earth Grant Program: Receive Up to $10,000 in Funding and Mentorship for Environmental, Community, and Cultural Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: DW Akademie and OHCHR Invite Journalists to Bangkok, Thailand for 2026 Migration and Scam Trafficking Reporting Training Program
summary: Journalists across South and Southeast Asia have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their reporting skills through a specialized international training program designed to improve coverage of migration, human rights, and scam trafficking. DW Akademie, in partnership with the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR), is inviting applications for the Navigating Narratives on Migration and Scam Trafficking Training for Journalists , which will take place in Bangkok, Thailand, from October 6–8, 2026 . The three-day, practice-oriented training will bring together journalists from selected countries across South and Southeast Asia to explore responsible migration reporting while addressing the growing challenge of scam-related exploitation. Participants will gain practical knowledge, enhance their storytelling skills, and learn how to report ethically on vulnerable populations without compromising their dignity or safety. Applications are currently open, and interested journalists must submit their applications before July 27, 2026 . About the Training Program Migration continues to be one of the world s most complex issues, intersecting with human rights, economic opportunities, technology, organized crime, and public policy. Unfortunately, migration is also increasingly linked to online scams, trafficking networks, misinformation, and disinformation campaigns that exploit vulnerable populations. Recognizing these challenges, DW Akademie and the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR) have developed a comprehensive regional training aimed at equipping journalists with practical reporting skills that contribute to accurate, ethical, and human-centered journalism. Rather than focusing solely on theoretical discussions, this workshop emphasizes hands-on learning and practical application. Participants will work through real-world reporting scenarios, engage with experienced trainers, and develop strategies for reporting on migration issues responsibly. The training seeks to improve public understanding of migration while helping journalists avoid harmful stereotypes and misleading narratives that often surround migration reporting. Training Dates and Venue The training will be held as follows: Location: Bangkok, Thailand Training Dates: October 6–8, 2026 Over three intensive days, participants will collaborate with fellow journalists from across the region, exchange experiences, and build professional networks that support cross-border reporting on migration and human rights issues. What Participants Will Learn The workshop has been carefully designed to address today s most pressing journalism challenges surrounding migration and scam trafficking. Participants will explore topics including: 1. Responsible Migration Reporting The training will help journalists: Produce balanced and evidence-based migration stories. Improve reporting accuracy on migration issues. Avoid sensationalism when covering vulnerable communities. Report in ways that respect the dignity and human rights of migrants. Understand ethical considerations when interviewing survivors and affected communities. 2. Scam Trafficking and Human Exploitation Participants will gain a deeper understanding of: Scam-related exploitation. Human trafficking connected to online scam operations. Cross-border criminal networks. The experiences of individuals trapped in scam compounds. Reporting approaches that protect victims from further harm. 3. Human Rights-Based Journalism A key focus of the workshop is ensuring that migration reporting reflects international human rights principles. Journalists will learn how to: Center human dignity in reporting. Minimize harm to interviewees. Avoid discriminatory language. Report on migration through a rights-based lens. 4. Migration, Disinformation, and Digital Media The workshop also examines how digital technologies influence migration narratives. Topics include: Misinformation surrounding migration. Disinformation campaigns targeting migrant communities. Algorithmic amplification of misleading content. The role of online platforms in shaping public opinion. Connections between digital ecosystems and online scam operations. Participants will explore practical tools for identifying false narratives and improving the quality of migration reporting in digital environments. Learning Resources Used During the Workshop The training is built upon two major resources developed by international experts. DW Akademie s Beyond Borders Toolkit Participants will work with the Beyond Borders Toolkit , a practical journalism resource developed by journalists for journalists. The toolkit provides guidance on: Ethical migration reporting. Story framing. Interview techniques. Responsible language choices. Human-centered storytelling. OHCHR Narrative Change Methodology The workshop also incorporates the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR) s Narrative Change Methodology . This framework helps journalists: Challenge harmful narratives. Promote inclusive storytelling. Highlight human rights perspectives. Encourage accurate public understanding of migration. Why This Training Matters Migration stories significantly influence public opinion and policy discussions. Poorly framed reporting can reinforce stereotypes, increase discrimination, and contribute to misinformation. This training seeks to address these challenges by helping journalists produce reporting that is: Accurate. Ethical. Evidence-based. Human rights-centered. Sensitive to vulnerable communities. Resistant to misinformation and disinformation. By improving reporting standards, journalists can contribute to more informed public conversations while supporting accountability and protecting affected populations. About the Organizers DW Akademie DW Akademie is Germany s leading organization for international media development. It works with journalists, media organizations, and educational institutions worldwide to strengthen independent journalism, freedom of expression, and access to reliable information. Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR) OHCHR is the principal United Nations office responsible for promoting and protecting human rights globally. The organization supports initiatives that strengthen human rights-based approaches across multiple sectors, including journalism and public communication. Project Background The training forms part of DW Akademie s Displacement and Dialogue Asia Project . The initiative is funded by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ) and aims to improve migration dialogue throughout Asia by supporting ethical journalism, informed public discourse, and responsible reporting practices. Why Journalists Should Apply Participants will benefit from: Practical journalism training. Hands-on reporting exercises. Regional networking opportunities. Exposure to international journalism methodologies. Improved understanding of migration reporting. Skills for reporting on scam trafficking. Human rights-based storytelling techniques. Knowledge on combating misinformation and disinformation. Cross-border collaboration with journalists from neighboring countries. The workshop offers an opportunity for journalists to strengthen both their professional skills and their contribution to responsible public-interest journalism across the region. Visit HERE For More Information About the Opportunity DW Akademie and OHCHR Invite Journalists to Bangkok, Thailand for 2026 Migration and Scam Trafficking Reporting Training Program Application Deadline Interested journalists should submit their applications before July 27, 2026 . Early application is encouraged, as the training is expected to attract significant interest from journalists across South and Southeast Asia. APPLY HERE Conclusion The Navigating Narratives on Migration and Scam Trafficking Training for Journalists represents an important opportunity for media professionals seeking to improve their reporting on migration, human rights, and online scam exploitation. Through practical learning, regional collaboration, and internationally recognized methodologies, participants will gain valuable tools for producing ethical, accurate, and impactful journalism. As migration-related issues continue to evolve across Asia, journalists play a critical role in shaping public understanding. This training equips them with the knowledge, skills, and ethical approaches needed to report responsibly while protecting the dignity of those most affected. Journalists from the eligible countries are encouraged to apply before the July 27, 2026 deadline and become part of a regional movement promoting responsible journalism. For more Global Conference Opportunites, visit OFY HERE The post DW Akademie and OHCHR Invite Journalists to Bangkok, Thailand for 2026 Migration and Scam Trafficking Reporting Training Program appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Royal Society – Research Ireland University Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded 8-Year Fellowship for Outstanding Early-Career Scientists in Ireland
summary: Outstanding early-career scientists seeking to establish themselves as independent research leaders now have an exceptional opportunity to secure long-term funding through the Royal Society – Research Ireland University Research Fellowship (URF) 2026 . This prestigious fellowship is designed to support talented researchers in the Republic of Ireland who have demonstrated exceptional scientific potential and are ready to build internationally competitive research careers. Jointly supported by the Royal Society and Research Ireland , the programme provides up to eight years of funding , enabling successful fellows to pursue ambitious research programmes while developing the skills, experience, and leadership needed to become permanent academic or research leaders. Open to researchers across the natural sciences—including biology, biomedical sciences, chemistry, engineering, mathematics, and physics—the fellowship offers one of the most significant long-term career development opportunities available to early-career scientists in Ireland. The application deadline is 9 September 2026 at 15:00 (local Irish time) . About the Royal Society – Research Ireland University Research Fellowship The Royal Society – Research Ireland University Research Fellowship (URF) is a flagship funding scheme aimed at nurturing the next generation of world-class scientific leaders in the Republic of Ireland. The fellowship enables exceptional researchers in the early stages of their careers to establish independent research programmes while working at universities or eligible research institutions. By providing sustained financial support over an extended period, the programme allows fellows to focus on high-quality research, develop internationally competitive research profiles, and position themselves for permanent academic appointments. Successful applicants will receive fellowships awarded by the Royal Society , with the costs fully funded by Research Ireland . The scheme reflects the shared commitment of both organizations to advancing scientific excellence, strengthening Ireland s research ecosystem, and supporting innovative discoveries that address global scientific challenges. Purpose of the Fellowship The primary objective of the University Research Fellowship is to help outstanding scientists transition from promising early-career researchers into internationally recognized independent research leaders. Specifically, the fellowship aims to: Support the development of independent scientific research careers. Enable researchers to establish their own research programmes. Foster scientific excellence across the natural sciences. Build Ireland s long-term research capacity. Prepare fellows for permanent academic and research leadership positions. Encourage internationally competitive research. Promote innovation and scientific discovery. Fellowship Duration One of the most attractive aspects of this opportunity is its long-term funding commitment. Successful fellows will receive support for: Up to 8 years of funding The funding structure includes: Initial funding period Mid-point assessment Final three years of funding awarded following successful assessment This long-term model provides researchers with the stability needed to undertake ambitious, high-impact scientific research. Funding Structure Successful candidates will receive: Fellowship awarded by the Royal Society Funding provided by Research Ireland Up to eight years of research funding Long-term support to establish an independent research programme The final three years of funding are contingent upon a successful mid-point review, ensuring continued progress and research excellence. Research Areas Supported The fellowship supports all fields of the natural sciences . Eligible disciplines include, but are not limited to: Biological Sciences Researchers working in: Biology Ecology Genetics Molecular biology Evolutionary biology Microbiology Plant sciences Zoology Biomedical Sciences Including: Medical sciences Biomedical research Human health Translational medicine Immunology Neuroscience Chemistry Research areas such as: Organic chemistry Analytical chemistry Physical chemistry Materials chemistry Environmental chemistry Engineering Including: Civil engineering Mechanical engineering Electrical engineering Chemical engineering Biomedical engineering Materials engineering Mathematics Areas including: Pure mathematics Applied mathematics Statistics Computational mathematics Mathematical modelling Physics Research fields including: Theoretical physics Experimental physics Astrophysics Condensed matter physics Quantum science Particle physics The fellowship also welcomes proposals from other natural science disciplines aligned with the programme s objectives. Why Apply? The Royal Society – Research Ireland University Research Fellowship is one of Ireland s premier career-development opportunities for early-career scientists. Successful fellows will benefit from: Long-term research funding Opportunity to establish an independent research group Increased research autonomy Career progression toward permanent academic positions International recognition through the Royal Society Enhanced research leadership experience Opportunities to build collaborations Increased competitiveness for future research funding Ability to undertake ambitious scientific projects Strong institutional support Career Development Opportunities Beyond financial support, fellows will have the opportunity to: Build an internationally competitive research profile. Develop leadership and supervisory experience. Mentor postgraduate students and junior researchers. Expand international research collaborations. Publish high-impact scientific papers. Increase research visibility. Strengthen applications for permanent university appointments. Eligibility The fellowship targets researchers who are: Outstanding scientists. In the early stages of their research careers. Demonstrating exceptional scientific promise. Ready to establish independent research programmes. Showing strong leadership potential. Applicants should have the potential to become internationally recognized leaders within their disciplines. Who Should Apply? This fellowship is ideal for: Early-career researchers Postdoctoral researchers Research scientists Academic researchers University-based scientists Emerging research leaders Scientists seeking independence in research Applicants should possess strong scientific achievements and demonstrate the capacity to build successful long-term research programmes. Fields Eligible for Funding The fellowship supports research across: Biology Biomedical Sciences Chemistry Engineering Mathematics Physics Other Natural Sciences Funding Timeline Call Launch 14 July 2026 Fellowship Duration Up to 8 years Mid-Point Assessment Funding for the final three years will be subject to a successful mid-term review. Application Process Prospective applicants are encouraged to: Carefully read the official scheme guidance notes. Review the eligibility requirements. Consult departmental contacts. Speak with current or former University Research Fellowship holders for advice and insights. Prepare all required application materials well before the submission deadline. Ensure nominated referees and Heads of Department complete their required documentation before the deadline. Applicants should note that the deadline also applies to: Nominated References Head of Department Statements Late submissions may not be accepted. Visit HERE to learn more and apply. Information and Support Applicants who require additional clarification after reviewing the scheme notes may contact the Royal Society University Research Fellowship team. Research Ireland also encourages prospective applicants to connect with current and former fellowship holders through their departments to gain valuable insight into preparing competitive applications. Important Dates Activity Date Call Launch 14 July 2026 Application Deadline 9 September 2026 (15:00 Irish Local Time) Fellowship Duration Up to 8 years Why This Fellowship Stands Out Several features distinguish this programme from many other research fellowships: Eight years of funding provides exceptional career stability. Joint backing by the Royal Society and Research Ireland offers significant prestige. Supports the transition to research independence. Covers all major natural science disciplines. Designed specifically for future research leaders. Provides a pathway toward permanent academic careers. Encourages ambitious, high-impact scientific research. For researchers aiming to establish themselves as internationally competitive scientists, this fellowship represents an outstanding career milestone. Tips for Preparing a Strong Application Applicants may strengthen their submissions by: Clearly articulating the originality and significance of their proposed research. Demonstrating independence from previous supervisors. Highlighting previous publications, research achievements, and scientific impact. Presenting a realistic long-term research vision. Securing strong institutional support. Working closely with referees to ensure timely submission of references. Reviewing the official guidance thoroughly before applying. Conclusion The Royal Society – Research Ireland University Research Fellowship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for outstanding early-career scientists to establish themselves as independent research leaders through one of Ireland s most prestigious long-term fellowship programmes. With funding of up to eight years, recognition from the Royal Society, and financial support from Research Ireland, the fellowship provides an ideal environment for ambitious researchers to pursue innovative scientific discoveries while building internationally competitive careers. Whether your expertise lies in biology, biomedical sciences, chemistry, engineering, mathematics, physics, or another natural science discipline, this fellowship provides the resources, stability, and prestige needed to make a lasting impact on the global scientific community. Interested applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their applications early and ensure all required documentation, references, and institutional statements are submitted before the 9 September 2026 deadline. For more global Fellowship and Research opportunities, visit OFY .
