title: WomenPower2030 Seed Grant 2026: Up to €5,000 for Women-Led Organizations in Nigeria and Sierra Leone Advancing Gender-Just Climate Action and Sustainable Development
summary: The Women Environmental Programme (WEP) has officially opened applications for the WomenPower2030 Seed Grant 2026 , inviting eligible women-led and women’s rights organizations in Nigeria and Sierra Leone to apply for funding of up to €5,000 to implement innovative initiatives that advance gender equality, climate justice, and sustainable development. The grant forms part of the WomenPower2030: Feminist Accelerate Actions for Sustainable Development initiative, a global partnership supported through a Financial Framework Partnership Agreement (FFPA) between the European Union and a consortium of leading feminist organizations. Through this funding opportunity, selected organizations will implement projects that strengthen women s leadership, influence public policies, promote gender-responsive climate action, and contribute to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). About the WomenPower2030 Project WomenPower2030 is an international initiative designed to strengthen the participation and leadership of women, girls, and marginalized groups in sustainable development and climate governance. The project seeks to ensure that feminist organizations and women s civil society organizations play an active role in designing, implementing, and monitoring policies and programmes that promote inclusive and gender-just development. The overall objective is to advance the rights and leadership of women, girls, and marginalized communities while accelerating progress toward sustainable development. Consortium Behind the Initiative WomenPower2030 is implemented through a partnership led by Women Engage for a Common Future (WECF) alongside four international feminist organizations: Women Environmental Programme (WEP) African Women s Development and Communication Network (FEMNET) Asia Pacific Forum on Women, Law and Development (APWLD) Fundación para Estudio e Investigación de la Mujer (FEIM) Together, these organizations work to amplify feminist leadership and influence sustainable development policies at national, regional, and global levels. Seed Grant Objectives The seed grants are intended to empower women-led organizations to develop and implement impactful initiatives that contribute to the goals of WomenPower2030. Successful projects should strengthen women s participation in decision-making, influence policy reforms, promote sustainable solutions, and improve gender-responsive governance. Projects are expected to create measurable impact rather than focusing solely on awareness campaigns. Priority Areas of Support Applicants may propose projects addressing one or more of the following thematic areas: 1. Policy Monitoring and Policy Dialogue Projects that: Monitor government policies. Engage policymakers. Advocate for gender-responsive policies. Promote accountability in sustainable development. 2. Education and Awareness Initiatives that raise awareness on: Gender equality. Climate action. Sustainable development. Women s rights. However, awareness activities alone are not sufficient to qualify for funding. 3. Movement Building Projects that: Strengthen women s movements. Build coalitions. Increase collaboration among feminist organizations. Promote collective advocacy. 4. Demonstration of Sustainable Solutions Initiatives showcasing: Practical sustainable development solutions. Community-based innovations. Environmentally sustainable services. Gender-responsive climate interventions. 5. Women s Leadership and Capacity Building Projects focusing on: Women s leadership training. Gender-specific capacity development. Monitoring and accountability. Skills strengthening. 6. Tracking SDG and Climate Policies Projects that monitor: Sustainable Development Goal implementation. Climate policies. Government budgets. Gender-responsive budgeting. 7. Social Media and Mainstream Media Campaigns Strategic communication initiatives that strengthen advocacy through: Digital campaigns. Media engagement. Public awareness linked to policy influence. Important Funding Priorities The Women Environmental Programme emphasizes that priority will be given to projects that: Influence public policies benefiting women. Demonstrate sustainable and equitable solutions. Promote gender-responsive budgeting. Integrate women s needs into development planning. Applications focusing primarily on workshops or awareness-raising activities without broader policy or systems impact will not be considered. Grant Value Each successful organization may receive funding of up to: €5,000 The funding is intended to support the implementation of innovative projects aligned with the objectives of WomenPower2030. Project Duration Funded projects should be implemented within: 3 to 6 months Applicants should prepare realistic work plans that can be completed within this timeframe. Language of Application All applications must be submitted in: English Required Application Documents Eligible organizations must submit: Completed application form (Word format) Detailed budget (Excel format) Certificate of registration Letter of reference Evidence of previous work (reports, media coverage, testimonials, or related documentation compiled into one document) The organizers may request additional supporting information where necessary. Application Guidelines Applicants should note the following: Applications must not exceed 10 pages , excluding attachments. Only Word documents (.doc or .docx) will be accepted. Applications that fail to address the specified focus areas will not be considered. Only shortlisted or successful applicants may be contacted due to the expected high number of submissions. How to Apply Interested organizations should: Download the official application form. Complete the application in English. Prepare all required supporting documents. Attach the detailed budget and required organizational documents. Submit the application via email with the required subject line: WomenPower2030 seed-grant application Applications should be submitted to the official grants email provided by the Women Environmental Programme. Application Deadline Deadline: 31 July 2026 (23:59 WAT) Late applications are unlikely to be considered, and applicants are encouraged to submit well before the deadline. Why Organizations Should Apply The WomenPower2030 Seed Grant offers women-led organizations an opportunity to: Secure funding of up to €5,000. Strengthen advocacy for gender-responsive policies. Influence climate and sustainable development decision-making. Promote women s leadership. Demonstrate innovative sustainability solutions. Expand partnerships within regional and global feminist networks. Contribute to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals. Conclusion The WomenPower2030 Seed Grant 2026 presents an excellent opportunity for women-led organizations in Nigeria and Sierra Leone to access funding for initiatives that strengthen women s leadership, influence public policy, and advance gender-responsive climate action and sustainable development. Organizations with innovative ideas capable of creating lasting policy and community impact are encouraged to prepare strong applications and submit them before the 31 July 2026 deadline. For more information on this grant, visit here Discover more grant opportunities on OFY, here The post WomenPower2030 Seed Grant 2026: Up to €5,000 for Women-Led Organizations in Nigeria and Sierra Leone Advancing Gender-Just Climate Action and Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: PhD Scholarship at the University of Limerick, Ireland 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity in Applied Language Studies and Climate Journalism Research
summary: The University of Limerick (UL), Ireland , is inviting applications for a prestigious fully funded PhD Scholarship within the Centre for Applied Language Studies in the School of Modern Languages and Applied Linguistics . This four-year doctoral opportunity is designed for outstanding students with a passion for language, journalism, sociolinguistics, climate communication, and interdisciplinary research. The scholarship forms part of an ambitious research project titled Posthumanist Sociolinguistics and Journalistic Practices: Reporting on Climate Change in Irish and German Media. The project is funded by Research Ireland and the Irish Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and aims to transform understanding of how climate change is represented in mainstream journalism. Successful applicants will receive an annual stipend of €25,000 , payment of EU student tuition fees , extensive research training, conference participation opportunities, publication experience, and supervision from leading experts in the field. Scholarship Overview Climate change has become one of the defining global challenges of our time. As weather events become increasingly severe and environmental crises continue to intensify, journalists play an essential role in informing the public about climate issues and encouraging meaningful responses. Despite the growing importance of climate journalism, traditional media outlets across Europe have frequently been criticised for presenting climate change through predominantly human-centred perspectives. Researchers believe these reporting approaches may limit public understanding of the broader environmental crisis. This PhD project seeks to address these challenges by examining how Irish and German media organisations report on climate change and how language, images, and other communicative strategies influence public understanding of environmental issues. The successful PhD candidate will contribute significantly to this groundbreaking research while simultaneously developing an independent doctoral thesis. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive funding package that includes: Annual stipend of €25,000 Funding for 4 years of full-time doctoral study Payment of EU student tuition fees Opportunity to participate in: International conferences Career development programmes Academic workshops Collaborative publications Professional research training Supervision by experienced researchers at the University of Limerick Opportunity to work on a major internationally funded research project Important Note: Applicants from outside the European Union may be required to pay the difference between EU tuition fees and international tuition fees. Research Project Focus The scholarship is part of a wider comparative research project investigating climate journalism in Ireland and Germany. The research explores several important questions, including: How do journalists report climate change? How do newspapers frame environmental issues? What language is used when discussing climate protection? How do media representations influence public understanding of climate change? What differences exist between Irish and German reporting practices? How do political, cultural, and economic contexts shape climate journalism? The project combines qualitative and quantitative research methods to generate new insights into environmental communication. Responsibilities of the PhD Candidate The selected doctoral researcher will play a central role throughout the project. Primary responsibilities include: Compiling a large corpus of newspaper articles related to climate change Collecting articles from one Irish broadsheet newspaper and one German broadsheet newspaper Organising and preparing datasets for analysis Conducting corpus linguistic analysis Identifying linguistic patterns in climate reporting Examining anthropocentric news framing Performing detailed multimodal analysis of news reports Comparing climate reporting practices between Ireland and Germany Investigating cultural, economic, and political influences on media coverage Developing an independent doctoral thesis based on the research findings Academic Environment Throughout the four-year scholarship, the successful candidate will be based at the: Centre for Applied Language Studies School of Modern Languages and Applied Linguistics University of Limerick Ireland The researcher will work under the supervision of: Dr Sarah Josefine Schaefer (Principal Investigator) Professor Helen Kelly-Holmes In addition to direct supervision, the doctoral student will benefit from: Academic mentoring Research collaboration Networking opportunities Skills development workshops Career development programmes Opportunities for international conference participation Academic publishing experience Essential Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements: Demonstrate B2 (CEFR) level proficiency (or equivalent) in both: Written English Spoken English Written German Spoken German Hold an honours undergraduate degree (or equivalent) with at least 2.1 classification in one of the following or a closely related discipline: Linguistics German English Journalism Related fields Preferred Qualifications Although not mandatory, applicants with the following qualifications will be highly competitive: Master s degree in: Applied Linguistics Sociolinguistics Journalism Sociology Social Sciences Related disciplines Experience using qualitative research methods Experience using quantitative research methods Knowledge of discourse analysis Experience with linguistic corpus analysis Strong interest in interdisciplinary research Required Application Documents Applicants must submit a complete application consisting of the following documents: Cover Letter (maximum 1,000 words) explaining: Why you are interested in the project Your suitability for doctoral research What you would contribute to the research project Curriculum Vitae (CV) including: Educational background Employment history Relevant training Research experience Academic Writing Sample such as: Bachelor s thesis chapter Master s thesis chapter Published academic article Other scholarly writing Contact information for two academic referees Official university transcripts Certified English translations of transcripts if the originals are not in English or German Application document should be sent to this email: helen.kelly.holmes@ul.ie Selection Process Applications will undergo a competitive review process. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to participate in online interviews during September 2026 . The selection committee will assess: Academic excellence Research potential Suitability for interdisciplinary research Communication skills Motivation for pursuing doctoral study Alignment with the objectives of the project Important Dates Application Deadline: 17:00 Irish local time, Monday, 14 September 2026 Online Interviews: September 2026 Scholarship Start Date: 1 December 2026 Scholarship Duration: 4 years (full-time only) Why Apply? This scholarship provides an outstanding opportunity for aspiring researchers to contribute to internationally significant research examining how media shape public understanding of climate change. Participants will gain advanced expertise in: Applied Linguistics Sociolinguistics Climate Communication Journalism Studies Corpus Linguistics Discourse Analysis Multimodal Research Comparative Media Studies In addition to receiving generous financial support, doctoral researchers will build strong international academic networks, publish scholarly research, and develop highly transferable research skills for careers in academia, policy, journalism, communication, and beyond. Visit HERE to learn more about PhD Scholarship at the University of Limerick, Ireland 2026 Conclusion The University of Limerick s PhD Scholarship in the Centre for Applied Language Studies offers an exceptional opportunity for graduates interested in language, journalism, climate communication, and interdisciplinary research. With four years of funding, an annual stipend of €25,000, tuition support, expert supervision, and opportunities for professional development, this scholarship provides an excellent foundation for a successful research career. Prospective applicants should ensure that all required application documents are prepared and submitted before 17:00 Irish local time on Monday, 14 September 2026 , to be considered for this highly competitive doctoral opportunity. For more global scholarship opportunities, visit HERE The post PhD Scholarship at the University of Limerick, Ireland 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity in Applied Language Studies and Climate Journalism Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Rithm Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid Youth Leadership Fellowship for Young Changemakers Shaping Human Connection in the Age of AI
summary: Artificial Intelligence is transforming nearly every aspect of modern life—from education and work to communication and relationships. While AI presents exciting opportunities, it also raises important questions about loneliness, digital well-being, and the future of human connection. To ensure young people are not just passive users of technology but active architects of a more connected future, The Rithm Project has opened applications for its Rithm Fellowship 2026–2027 . This paid fellowship is designed for five exceptional young leaders aged 18 to 22 who are passionate about shaping conversations around AI, technology, mental health, education, and human relationships. Fellows will receive a USD $2,000 stipend , work alongside researchers and innovators, contribute to national conversations, and help influence how society approaches human connection in the age of artificial intelligence. About The Rithm Project The Rithm Project is a mission-driven organization dedicated to building a future where human connection thrives alongside artificial intelligence. Rather than viewing AI solely through the lens of productivity or technological advancement, the organization focuses on helping young people cultivate meaningful relationships, empathy, belonging, and community in an increasingly digital world. The organization conducts youth-centered research, develops educational tools and resources, convenes leaders across sectors, and creates opportunities for young people to influence conversations around AI and society. Its vision is to ensure that the next generation helps shape technologies that strengthen rather than weaken human relationships. About the Rithm Fellowship The Rithm Fellowship is a youth leadership programme where participants play an active role in shaping The Rithm Project s work. Rather than simply attending workshops, fellows become trusted collaborators whose perspectives help guide research, influence organizational strategy, and shape products designed for young people. The fellowship intentionally brings together a diverse cohort of five individuals from different backgrounds, experiences, and geographic locations. The goal is to create a community where varied lived experiences contribute to richer conversations about AI, connection, and the future. The fellowship runs from September 2026 through May 2027 , combining leadership development, collaborative project work, public engagement, and opportunities to represent youth voices in national conversations. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive a range of professional and leadership development opportunities, including: USD $2,000 stipend for participation. Opportunity to influence national conversations on AI and human connection. Direct collaboration with researchers, educators, technologists, and mental health experts. Hands-on experience contributing to research and innovative projects. Opportunities to represent youth perspectives at conferences, workshops, and speaking engagements. Leadership development in facilitation, communication, and collaboration. Networking with a multigenerational community of changemakers. Participation in exclusive Rithm Project events and summits. Experience working with one of the leading organizations exploring AI and youth well-being. What Fellows Will Do The fellowship is divided into two primary areas of responsibility. 1. Youth Leadership and Public Representation (Approximately 60%) Fellows will: Represent youth perspectives during internal strategy discussions. Participate in conferences, workshops, and public events. Engage with educators, researchers, policymakers, funders, and mental health professionals. Facilitate discussions that encourage diverse viewpoints. Travel independently within the United States for selected fellowship activities. 2. Research and Project Contributions (Approximately 40%) Participants will also: Contribute to youth-focused research. Pilot educational resources and digital tools. Help shape The Rithm Project s products and initiatives. Share lived experiences that inform future programmes. Collaborate on projects addressing AI, belonging, and social connection. Develop practical ideas that can influence future systems and policies. Who Is the Fellowship Looking For? The Rithm Project is seeking young people who are thoughtful, curious, and willing to engage in meaningful conversations about technology and society. Ideal candidates are individuals who: Have lived experiences or insights related to AI and human connection. Care deeply about strengthening communities and relationships. Are confident sharing ideas while remaining open to different perspectives. Possess empathy and emotional intelligence. Enjoy storytelling and communicating complex ideas in accessible ways. Demonstrate initiative and a willingness to contribute to collaborative projects. Have a growth mindset and are eager to learn from feedback. Can think critically while translating ideas into practical action. Time Commitment The fellowship is intentionally designed to fit alongside academic or professional commitments. Participants should expect to dedicate: Approximately 7–10 hours per month . Additional time for occasional travel and public events. Successful applicants must be available for the following mandatory in-person events in Denver, Colorado: October 16–18, 2026 – Fellowship Kickoff. May 10–13, 2027 – Human Connection and AI Summit. Why You Should Apply The Rithm Fellowship is more than a leadership programme—it is an opportunity to help shape one of the most important conversations of our time. As AI continues to transform education, work, and relationships, young people are uniquely positioned to influence how technology is developed and used. By joining this fellowship, participants will not only develop leadership and communication skills but also contribute to research, public dialogue, and practical solutions that prioritize empathy, connection, and human flourishing. Whether your passion lies in education, technology, mental health, public policy, or community organizing, this fellowship offers a platform to make your voice heard and collaborate with leaders committed to building a healthier digital future. Application Deadline Applications close on 15 July 2026. Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early. How to Apply Applications must be submitted through the official Rithm Fellowship webpage . For more information about The Rithm Project and its mission, visit HERE Click here to discover more global fellowship opportunites on OFY. The post Rithm Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid Youth Leadership Fellowship for Young Changemakers Shaping Human Connection in the Age of AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IOC Sustainability Trainee Internship 2026 in Switzerland: Launch Your Global Sustainability Career with the International Olympic Committee
summary: If you are a recent graduate passionate about sustainability, climate action, and creating a positive global impact through sport, the International Olympic Committee (IOC) is offering an exciting opportunity to begin your international career. Applications are now open for the IOC Sustainability Trainee Internship , a 12-month full-time traineeship based in Lausanne, Switzerland . Hosted within the Olympic Games Department , this internship provides young professionals with the opportunity to contribute to sustainability initiatives that support one of the world s most recognized international organizations. As the guardian of the Olympic Games and the leader of the Olympic Movement, the IOC works with stakeholders across the globe to promote sustainable development, environmental responsibility, and social impact through sport. Successful candidates will work alongside experienced sustainability professionals while contributing to projects related to climate action, biodiversity, circular economy practices, sustainable procurement, and international partnerships. This is an outstanding opportunity for graduates seeking practical experience within a multicultural environment while helping shape the future of sustainable sport. About the International Olympic Committee The International Olympic Committee (IOC) is an independent international organization responsible for overseeing the Olympic Games and promoting Olympic values worldwide. Beyond organizing the world s largest sporting event, the IOC collaborates with governments, international organizations, National Olympic Committees, International Federations, athletes, and global partners to promote peace, inclusion, education, sustainability, and development through sport. The IOC maintains partnerships with organizations such as: United Nations (UN) United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) International Federations National Olympic Committees Worldwide Olympic Partners Working at the IOC offers employees exposure to a truly international environment with colleagues representing more than 70 nationalities , making it one of the most diverse organizations in global sport. About the Sustainability Trainee Role The Sustainability Trainee will become part of the IOC Sustainability Team within the Olympic Games Department and will support the implementation of projects aligned with the IOC Sustainability Strategy. The internship provides hands-on experience in planning, coordinating, monitoring, and delivering sustainability initiatives that influence not only the IOC as an organization but also the Olympic Games and the wider Olympic Movement. This role is ideal for graduates who wish to combine their passion for environmental sustainability with sport, international development, and project management. Key Responsibilities As a Sustainability Trainee, you will support numerous sustainability initiatives across different areas. Your responsibilities will include: Climate and Environmental Sustainability Support projects related to climate action and climate adaptation. Contribute to biodiversity initiatives. Assist with sustainable procurement activities. Support circular economy projects across the Olympic Movement. Partnership Support Collaborate with sustainability partnerships including: Sport for Climate Action Framework Sport for Nature Framework Event Coordination Assist in organizing sustainability-related meetings and events involving: International Federations National Olympic Committees IOC Commercial Partners UNEP IUCN UNFCCC Other institutional partners Tasks may include: Preparing invitations Coordinating logistics Creating presentations Facilitating meetings Following up on action items Reporting and Project Monitoring The trainee will also: Prepare sustainability reports. Develop dashboards measuring sustainability progress. Support carbon footprint reporting. Assist with procurement sustainability reporting. Project Management The successful candidate will help coordinate flagship initiatives including: IOC Climate Action Awards Athlete Sustainability Ambassador Programme In addition, the trainee will provide support for cross-functional sustainability projects across various IOC departments. What You Will Gain Working at the IOC provides far more than technical experience. Benefits include: Twelve months of international professional experience. Opportunity to work at IOC headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland. Exposure to global sustainability strategies. Practical experience managing international projects. Collaboration with leading sustainability experts. Experience working with United Nations agencies. Networking with professionals from more than 70 countries. Flexible and rewarding work environment. Competitive employee benefits. Health and wellness initiatives. Opportunity to contribute to global climate action through sport. Professional growth within one of the world s most prestigious international organizations. Qualifications Applicants should possess: A Bachelor s degree (minimum) in Sustainability, Sustainable Development, Environmental Studies, or a related discipline. Approximately one year of professional experience in sustainability or a similar field. Fluency in English. French or another language is considered an advantage. Strong Microsoft 365 skills including: Word Excel PowerPoint Outlook Understanding of sustainability principles. Knowledge of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Familiarity with LinkedIn and other social media platforms. Desired Skills and Personal Attributes The IOC is looking for candidates who demonstrate: Reliability and professionalism. Excellent planning and organizational abilities. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Critical thinking. Initiative and proactive problem-solving. Ability to prioritize multiple assignments. Excellent teamwork skills. Respect for diversity and inclusion. Commitment to ethical conduct. Passion for sustainability and the Olympic values. Why You Should Apply The IOC Sustainability Trainee Internship is one of the most prestigious early-career opportunities available in sustainability and international sport. It offers graduates the chance to work on projects with global environmental significance while collaborating with leading organizations such as UNEP and UNFCCC. Beyond gaining practical experience, trainees become part of the Olympic Movement, helping deliver initiatives that contribute to climate action, biodiversity conservation, sustainable procurement, and environmental leadership across international sport. Whether your long-term ambition is to work with international organizations, the United Nations, NGOs, governments, or sustainability consulting firms, this traineeship provides exceptional exposure and career-building opportunities. Application Deadline No specific application deadline has been announced. Applications are currently open, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to the high level of international competition. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official International Olympic Committee careers portal . Learn more about this internship opportunity here Click here to discover more global internship opportunities on OFY. The post IOC Sustainability Trainee Internship 2026 in Switzerland: Launch Your Global Sustainability Career with the International Olympic Committee appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
